sanskrit:
कर्मणो ह्यपि बोद्धव्यं बोद्धव्यं च विकर्मणः।
अकर्मणश्च बोद्धव्यं गहना कर्मणो गतिः॥
transliteration:
karmaṇo hy api boddhavyaṁ boddhavyaṁ ca vikarmaṇaḥ |
akarmaṇaś ca boddhavyaṁ gahanā karmaṇo gatiḥ ||
literal_meaning: Action, indeed, must be understood; forbidden action must also be understood; and inaction must be understood as well—deep and difficult is the path of action.
contextual_meaning: Krishna warns Arjuna that the psyche cannot rely on simplistic labels of ‘doing’ or ‘not-doing’; every choice contains hidden karmic loops that only become visible through disciplined self-inquiry.
psychological_state: Cognitive dissonance—Arjuna confronts the limits of black-and-white moral reasoning and feels the vertigo of ethical nuance.
trigger_scenario: A project manager realizes that both firing an under-performer (action) and keeping them on (inaction) will generate unforeseen harm; paralysis sets in because the mind cannot map the long-term ripple effects.
anchor_text: Discern action, inaction, and forbidden action through disciplined self-inquiry.
